gaps.title: Tenant isolation vulnerability in variant capability service
gaps.breaks: One tenant can see or modify another tenant's feature configurations
gaps.scenario: Tenant A requests list_capabilities with instance_id from Tenant B, receiving Tenant B's feature configurations instead of their own
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in variant_capability_service.py by having two different tenants attempt to access each other's feature configurations and ensuring only the correct tenant's data is returned

gaps.title: Race condition in ticket counter increment/decrement
gaps.breaks: Multiple simultaneous requests to increment_active_tickets could cause incorrect ticket counts
gaps.scenario: Tenant A has 1999 tickets, 10 concurrent requests each increment by 1, but system only counts 1-2 increments instead of 10
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent requests to increment_active_tickets and decrement_active_tickets in variant_instance_service.py to verify atomic operations and correct counting under high load

gaps.title: JSON parsing vulnerability in channel assignment
gaps.breaks: Malformed JSON in update_channel_assignment could crash the service
gaps.scenario: Tenant A sends update_channel_assignment with invalid JSON, causing the service to throw an unhandled exception and affecting all tenants
gaps.prompt: Write a test that sends various malformed JSON payloads to update_channel_assignment in variant_instance_service.py and verifies the service handles these gracefully without crashing

gaps.title: Namespace collision during instance registration
gaps.breaks: Two instances with same configuration could generate identical Redis/Celery namespaces
gaps.scenario: Tenant A and Tenant B both register instances with identical parameters, causing operations on one instance to affect the other
gaps.prompt: Write a test that registers multiple instances with similar configurations and verifies that their generated Redis/Celery namespaces are unique and properly isolated

gaps.title: Silent failure in batch update capabilities
gaps.breaks: Some updates in batch_update_capabilities succeed while others fail, but all return success status
gaps.scenario: Tenant A attempts to batch update 5 features, 3 succeed but 2 fail due to validation errors, but system reports all 5 succeeded
gaps.prompt: Write a test that performs a batch_update_capabilities with mixed valid and invalid feature updates and verifies the system accurately reports which succeeded and which failed

gaps.title: Instance state inconsistency after deletion
gaps.breaks: References to deleted instances still exist in other services
gaps.scenario: Tenant A deletes an instance, but get_least_loaded_instance still returns it or get_instance_for_channel tries to access its configuration
gaps.prompt: Write a test that deletes an instance and then verifies all other services properly handle this deletion by not returning or referencing the deleted instance

gaps.title: Capacity overflow not properly handled
gaps.breaks: Operations that would exceed capacity limits are not properly validated
gaps.scenario: Tenant A has 1999/2000 tickets, attempts to increment by 2, and system allows it to go to 2001 instead of rejecting
gaps.prompt: Write a test that attempts various operations that would exceed capacity limits in variant_instance_service.py and verifies the system properly rejects these operations with appropriate error messages

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 7

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Tenant isolation vulnerability in variant capability service
What breaks: One tenant can see or modify another tenant's feature configurations
Real scenario: Tenant A requests list_capabilities with instance_id from Tenant B, receiving Tenant B's feature configurations instead of their own
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in variant_capability_service.py by having two different tenants attempt to access each other's feature configurations and ensuring only the correct tenant's data is returned

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Race condition in ticket counter increment/decrement
What breaks: Multiple simultaneous requests to increment_active_tickets could cause incorrect ticket counts
Real scenario: Tenant A has 1999 tickets, 10 concurrent requests each increment by 1, but system only counts 1-2 increments instead of 10
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent requests to increment_active_tickets and decrement_active_tickets in variant_instance_service.py to verify atomic operations and correct counting under high load

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: JSON parsing vulnerability in channel assignment
What breaks: Malformed JSON in update_channel_assignment could crash the service
Real scenario: Tenant A sends update_channel_assignment with invalid JSON, causing the service to throw an unhandled exception and affecting all tenants
AI agent prompt: Write a test that sends various malformed JSON payloads to update_channel_assignment in variant_instance_service.py and verifies the service handles these gracefully without crashing

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Namespace collision during instance registration
What breaks: Two instances with same configuration could generate identical Redis/Celery namespaces
Real scenario: Tenant A and Tenant B both register instances with identical parameters, causing operations on one instance to affect the other
AI agent prompt: Write a test that registers multiple instances with similar configurations and verifies that their generated Redis/Celery namespaces are unique and properly isolated

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: Silent failure in batch update capabilities
What breaks: Some updates in batch_update_capabilities succeed while others fail, but all return success status
Real scenario: Tenant A attempts to batch update 5 features, 3 succeed but 2 fail due to validation errors, but system reports all 5 succeeded
AI agent prompt: Write a test that performs a batch_update_capabilities with mixed valid and invalid feature updates and verifies the system accurately reports which succeeded and which failed

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Instance state inconsistency after deletion
What breaks: References to deleted instances still exist in other services
Real scenario: Tenant A deletes an instance, but get_least_loaded_instance still returns it or get_instance_for_channel tries to access its configuration
AI agent prompt: Write a test that deletes an instance and then verifies all other services properly handle this deletion by not returning or referencing the deleted instance

GAP 7
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Capacity overflow not properly handled
What breaks: Operations that would exceed capacity limits are not properly validated
Real scenario: Tenant A has 1999/2000 tickets, attempts to increment by 2, and system allows it to go to 2001 instead of rejecting
AI agent prompt: Write a test that attempts various operations that would exceed capacity limits in variant_instance_service.py and verifies the system properly rejects these operations with appropriate error messages
